page: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. This configuration provides 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, supported by 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance features 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, establishing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps, with no bundled local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, with strong results in Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is in the top 25%, though large-block read and write speeds fall into the average tier. The instance also demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded compression, image processing, text processing, and small-model LLM inference. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and database operations remain in the average category.

From a resource density perspective, the r8a.4xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. A key tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency compared to instances with local SSDs. Additionally, while single-core performance is excellent, parallel compute scaling is moderate, as indicated by average multi-core benchmark scores.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, and memory-bound data processing. It is also well-suited for text processing, image processing, and small-scale language model inference tasks that benefit from high single-core performance and fast DDR5 memory.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 9R45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, but contains no local storage or GPU resources. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance, excellent small-block memory bandwidth, and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core scaling and large-block memory transfers align with average tiers. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, caching layers, and text or image processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for memory-intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and small-block memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU resources included

Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing
